CRITERIA: "Major Inclusion Criteria:~Hospitalization for an acute coronary syndrome (ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction, non-ST-segment elevation myocardial infarction or unstable angina pectoris) with planned invasive strategy~Major Exclusion Criteria:~1. intolerance of or allergy to ticagrelor or prasugrel~2. history of any stroke, transient ischemic attack or intracranial bleeding~3. known intracranial neoplasm, intracranial arteriovenous malformation or intracranial aneurysm~4. active bleeding...

NCT02270242.name: TWILIGHT
NCT02270242.group: High-risk PCI patients, ticagrelor monotherapy vs ticagrelor+aspirin after 3 mo DAPT - PRIMARY endpoint BARC 2/3/5 bleeding at 12 mo (Mehran 2019 NEJM 381:2032). Raw counts updated 2026-05-05 from MACE 135/137 to BARC 141/250 to match the headline publishedHR 0.56.

allOutcomes.title: Composite of death, MI, or stroke at 15 mo (key secondary; ticagrelor mono vs +ASA)

NCT02270242.snippet: Source: Mehran R et al. NEJM 2019;381:2032-2042 (TWILIGHT).

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 18 Years; healthy volunteers: f. NMA_CONFIG.outcome_label: Stroke or systemic embolism (trial-primary; ITT or modified ITT)
NMA_CONFIG.note: k=4 DOAC-vs-Warfarin trials. Warfarin-star network (no head-to-head between DOACs — transitivity supports indirect DOAC-vs-DOAC comparisons via shared Warfarin control). Matches Ruff 2014 Lancet landmark NMA canonical set.

title: RapidMeta Cardio | ACS Antiplatelet NMA (Clopidogrel / Ticagrelor / Prasugrel) v1.0
